channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large gap by road drain, 13th July
item.description:
This has been here for a week or so now, the damage this could cause and is also a danger to road users, including pedestrians as at any point it could cause a large hole

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Warwick Road, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black bagged dumped, 13th July
item.description:
Please remove

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32, Elcot Avenue, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bins, 13th July
item.description:
The bins outside what was the Standard Pub are overflowing. There is also a huge black bin there also full which shouldn’t be on a residential street. The smell is appalling and there are flies everywhere. Please can the bins be emptied and the large black bin be removed. This is an eyesore on a residential street and will encourage fly tipping
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pelton Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement and bus shelter blocked., 13th July
item.description:
The shelter and the pavement where one alights from the bus are blocked by cones and barriers left possibly from previous road works. There is no way a pushchair or wheelchair can pass without going in the road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bourne Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bins, 13th July
item.description:
Two large bins have been dumped in the road for two weeks. This is causing public danger to both cars and pedestrians. It is also attracting fly tippers and anyone who wants to dump their rubbish. Please do not tell me this is not the councils responsibility they are currently on council property THE ROAD. If they are not emptied and removed there will be yet another rat problem and probably a traffic or pedestrian accident. The bins belong to a company that residents have complained about before and nothing was done by the council. The have now moved and left a public hazard.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sherman Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unclear and confusing markings for cycle lane - Melford Rd, 13th July
item.description:
The section of Melford Rd between Underhill and Lordship Lane is one way for cars, but two way for bikes. The cycle lane heading from Lordship to Underhill isn't clearly marked, the road markings are faded and the cycle lane itself is only briefly marked out at the start and end of the road. Along the road there is just the odd cycle marking which is faded and easy for motorists to miss. You have bikes riding against the flow of traffic and there isn't a lot of space. It is very easy for motorists to miss the markings and think that the cyclists are doing the wrong thing when it is actually a cycle lane. The problem is made worse by the parking arrangement on the street which allows parking on both sides. Cars are effectively crossing the oncoming cycle traffic to park on the RHS of the road and it causes the road to narrow down significantly, particularly around the bend. There is a natural tendency for motorists to drive in the middle of the available space between the 2 sets of parked , cars, really pinching off the cyclists. I think the lowest cost improvement that you could do would be to redo the road markings with clearly defined cycle lane (continuous line with a different colour for the cycle lane). It would make it more obvious for drivers that they have to stay over to the left because bikes are coming the other way. If you could also look at some of the parking arrangements around the narrower parts of the road, that would also help, but I'm less hopeful of that happening.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Melford Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 13th July
item.description:
Once again fly tipping on the corner of Bastion Road & the junction of Wickham Lane. About time Greenwich Council installs CCTV to stop this persistent fly tipping.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bastion Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 13th July
item.description:
Once again someone has fly tipped a record turntable and electrical leads by the rubbish bin on the pathway at the bottom of the T GREEN between Bastion Road & Rutherglen Road. About time the council putts CCTV where regular fly tipping occurs.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bastion Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bikes. One Lime, one Voi. Double whammy, 13th July
item.description:
Dumped Voi bike with its friend, a Lime bike. Next to steps in One Tree Hill nature reserve near the ‘Honor Oak’ tree.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): One Tree Hill, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lime bikes block pavement, 13th July
item.description:
Two lime bikes have been parked on the pavement to completely block it from any use at the end of Truman's Road at junction with A10 Road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 35-39, Pellerin Road, Shacklewell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Post 31 on Faraday Avenue is out, 13th July
item.description:
Post 31 on Faraday Avenue on the T junction with Walton Road, Sidcup was out Thursday 9th July at 23:00.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 121, Faraday Avenue, Albany Park, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bikes. One Lime, one Voi. Double whammy, 13th July
item.description:
Dumped Lime bike. In woods at One Tree Hill nature reserve, bottom of steps near the ‘Honor Oak’ tree. Couldn’t see a number but I’ve tried to photograph the qr code. Voi bike reported separately.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): One Tree Hill, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
